title: Make Bureau candidate intake and task promotion operator-native
goal: Let ChatGPT through Grabowski record, assess, deduplicate, propose and publish durable Bureau tasks through typed, idempotent, source-bound and receipt-producing machine surfaces, while the user remains observer and steersman and is never required to run shell commands, inspect files or edit Registry JSON.

approval.note: Implementing T017 changes Bureau and Grabowski source repositories and therefore uses the runtime-enforced repository_mutation/operator gate. The publication operation implemented by T017 must independently evaluate registry_mutation with reviewed_plan before writing durable Registry task material.

acceptance.assertion: Machine-readable contracts and operator guidance name ChatGPT through Grabowski as the executing operator and the user as observer and steersman. The operator owns live observation, planning, registration, execution, verification and reporting; the user owns goals, meaning, priorities, risk tolerance, approvals, intervention and abort decisions.

One typed call records an append-only candidate with stable candidate ID, idempotency key, source kind, source locator or opaque reference, source digest when available, observed-at timestamp, repository binding, desired outcome, catalog-validation status and explicit non-claims. Repeated identical calls return the existing identity without a duplicate effect.

One typed read or plan call returns exact duplicate findings, bounded semantic similarity candidates, source freshness, target initiative, proposed scope and claims, risk and approval class, missing acceptance evidence and a decision of promote, merge, refine, defer or drop. Similarity never merges, closes or suppresses work automatically.

A task proposal binds candidate identity and current event, source digests, exact Registry commit and tree, target task path, complete task JSON, task JSON hash, proposed diff hash, duplicate evidence, unresolved fields, approval requirement and review status. Generic placeholder acceptance, capabilities or claims are rejected unless explicitly justified. Review authority is policy-bound: operator self-review is valid where the active contract permits it; the user is involved only when a steering, cost, safety, privacy or irreversibility gate explicitly requires human approval.

A typed publication call independently evaluates the runtime-backed registry_mutation approval rule and accepts only a reviewed proposal with unchanged plan bytes and Registry base. It acquires exact Bureau file and effect-gate leases, creates an isolated branch and pull request, validates the exact diff, records a durable receipt and releases leases. The implementation task's repository_mutation/operator approval does not satisfy or bypass this publication gate. It performs no queue mutation, claim, dispatch, merge, deployment or task verification.

The primary success path requires no human shell command, filesystem navigation, manual JSON edit, status aggregation or copy-paste review ceremony. CLI commands remain implementation transport and bounded diagnostic fallback, not the product interface.
All failures use stable machine codes with retryability, effect_started, ambiguity and required_readback fields. Ambiguous publication triggers bounded authoritative readback and never blind retry.

The operator surface returns bounded compact results by default, supports detail expansion by stable identifiers, avoids prose parsing and whole-registry loading, and measures tool calls, bytes, latency, duplicate prevention and manual-intervention count against the existing CLI-only path.
Business rules live once in Bureau domain code. CLI and Grabowski tools are thin adapters; no HTTP service is added without a separately evidenced remote consumer.

A fresh ChatGPT/Grabowski session with no conversation memory can discover the typed route, record one candidate, assess it, produce a reviewed proposal fixture and exercise a no-effect publication preview without human operational assistance.

rollback.strategy: Disable the typed Grabowski adapters and retain the existing Bureau Live Register and reviewed promotion CLI paths; no candidate or Registry history is rewritten.

metadata.source_request: User clarified that ChatGPT is the operator and the user is observer and steersman; all built surfaces are primarily for operator consumption.
metadata.supersedes_assumption: CLI quality is not evaluated as human convenience. It is evaluated only as machine transport, deterministic fallback and implementation boundary.
reuses: Bureau Live Register candidate identity and append-only supersession
Grabowski typed tools, leases, readback and receipts
metadata.review_semantics: Review is an evidence and policy property, not a synonym for manual human operation.
metadata.approval_contract: The implementation task is gated as repository_mutation because it writes Bureau and Grabowski code. Durable candidate-to-Registry publication is a separate operation-level registry_mutation requiring reviewed_plan; neither gate substitutes for the other.

bureau.full_validate: 780 tests passed; Ruff, Systemkatalog boundary, Registry and StateStore valid

grabowski.full_validate: 2441 tests passed; 2 skipped; all remaining validation stages passed

cli.result: record -> assess(promote) -> propose -> review(reviewed) -> preview(no effect); exact review replay existing and idempotent

typed_mcp.result: fresh MCP process discovered 155 tools and executed record -> assess(promote) -> propose -> review(reviewed) -> preview(ready, effect_started=false); exact review replay existing and idempotent

efficiency_benchmark.interpretation: Typed MCP did not improve call count or latency in this run; measured benefit is typed discovery, exact authority boundary and machine-readable contract. Both paths prevented one duplicate review effect through idempotent exact replay.

verified_acceptance.role-contract: PASS: machine-readable operator context and fresh-session execution preserve ChatGPT-through-Grabowski as operator and user as observer/steersman.
verified_acceptance.typed-candidate-record: PASS: typed record returned stable candidate identity; idempotency behavior is covered by integration tests and receipts.
verified_acceptance.typed-candidate-assessment: PASS: typed assessment returned promote with bounded structured evidence.
verified_acceptance.reviewed-task-proposal: PASS: proposal was digest-bound and reviewed by the new hash-bound review operation with no caller-controlled reviewed_at.
verified_acceptance.operator-native-publication: PASS: Bureau publication adapter and approval/readback contract are implemented and validated; live acceptance exercised the required no-effect publication preview rather than mutating canonical Registry.
verified_acceptance.no-human-operations: PASS: fresh MCP process completed the required path with zero manual proposal edits or user operational assistance.
verified_acceptance.typed-failure-and-readback: PASS: stable failure/ambiguity/readback behavior is covered by Bureau integration/fault tests and Grabowski adapter contracts.
verified_acceptance.machine-efficiency: PASS: reproducible CLI-vs-typed benchmark records calls, payload proxies, latency, duplicate prevention and manual intervention count without claiming a speed improvement.
verified_acceptance.transport-neutral-core: PASS: Bureau owns business rules; CLI and Grabowski remain thin adapters and no new HTTP service was introduced.
verified_acceptance.fresh-session-adoption: PASS: independent fresh MCP client, oriented only from machine-readable operator context, discovered and executed the typed route through reviewed no-effect preview.

residual_followups.reason: Already-open client sessions may retain a pre-deployment/frozen tool schema even when the server runtime is updated. T002 already owns connector snapshot identity, stale-state detection and reconnection behavior, so no duplicate T023 task is created.

metadata.verification_note: Bureau PR #788 and Grabowski PR #332 are merged, both implementations passed their full validation and required CI, both runtimes were deployed with exact source identity, a fresh independent MCP client discovered the 155-tool contract and executed record/assess/propose/review/preview without conversation handoff or manual proposal edits, and the reproducible benchmark plus idempotent replay evidence are recorded. The residual stale-session schema observation is assigned to existing OMR-T002 and does not invalidate T017 fresh-session acceptance.
